summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a fire‑blazing 1978 show – Jerry’s guitar wails, U.S. Blues and Truckin’ roar, with only early‑set vocal dropouts.
review: The recording is a well‑remastered soundboard (sbd) that sounds crisp, loud and balanced, though the first two songs suffer from missing vocals and a few glitches at the start. Musically the concert is legendary – the second set is described as “unparalleled,” with scorching Truckin’, an incendiary U.S. Blues, a monster New Minglewood Blues, a ferocious Lazy Lightning > Supplication, and a flawless Row Jimmy. Jerry’s playing (wind‑mills, screaming solos) and the dual‑drum fireworks are repeatedly praised, while the only real blemishes are vocal fade‑outs on Jack Straw/Dire Wolf and an overly long Drum solo.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments about performance (70% weight) and a high‑quality soundboard with only minor technical hiccups (30% weight) lead to a near‑excellent overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered sbd sounds clear, loud and well balanced. Early‑set glitches: vocal drop‑outs on the first two tracks, a few click‑pop artifacts, but overall crisp with no boom or distortion.

poor_songs: Drums (too long for a few listeners)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and energetic, building momentum into an explosive second set. The show maintains high intensity throughout; only the early vocal issues slightly disrupt the flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly called "on fire," with superb guitar tone, wind‑mill theatrics, screaming U.S. Blues solo and a relentless improvisational drive. Vocals missing on opening songs due to mix problems.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned only in passing (lights‑off protest) and for solid bass foundation; no specific performance critique.
band_member_comments.Bob: General praise for vocal contributions (“great singing by all”), but no detailed comments on his rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards described as “great” early on, yet several reviewers note Keith’s piano disappearing during jams (e.g., Estimated Prophet).
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart receive high marks for heavy beats and dual‑drum excitement; a few listeners felt the Drum solo ran too long.
